world.title: Woman, Her Kids Refuse to Bury Deadbeat Husband Who Abandoned Them 26 Years Ago: "Baraka Zimeenda"
world.description: Issa Maulid, who abandoned his family 26 years ago, sought forgiveness online before his death. His poignant story sparks community reflection and sad responses.

world.description: Global oil prices jumped and U.S. stock futures slid ahead of Monday’s market open after the weekend saw Iran-backed Houthi militants launch ballistic missiles at Israel and 3,500 additional U.S. troops arrive in the Middle East as the conflict rounded one month.

world.description: China aligns its 15th Five-Year Plan with ASEAN, boosting cooperation in AI, green energy, and digital economy amid rising global energy challenges.

world.title: U.S. Embassy confirms temporary halt on immigrant visa issuance for affected countries
world.description: PATTAYA, Thailand – The U.S. Embassy in Thailand has clarified on social media that immigrant visa issuances are indefinitely suspended for nationals of 75 countries, including Thailand. This action follows a U.S. Department of State announcement, effective January 21, which reinforces an earlier directive. The suspension was reportedly implemented due to high public welfare usage […]

world.title: Middle East crisis live: Trump says he wants to ‘take the oil’ in Iran and could seize Kharg Island ‘easily’
world.description: US president tells Financial Times his ‘preference would be to take the oil’ but that ‘some stupid people back in the US say: why are you doing that?’

world.title: Hormuz tensions threaten Türkiye's food security
world.description: ​As geopolitical tensions in the Strait of Hormuz disrupt global supply chains, Turkish agriculture is hit hard. Farmers and experts warn that soaring fertilizer costs are now a direct threat to the country's food security.

business.title: Millat Tractors makes a big bet on Pakistan and beyond
business.description: The historic tractor manufacturer will become the first automotive company in Pakistan’s history to export its own brand to other countries. But even as they look towards expanding their horizons, challenges persist at home. Can a National Tractory Policy help address them?

business.title: In show of defiance, JBS workers in Greeley, Colorado extend strike into third week
business.description: The defiance by JBS management must be answered by workers through a deepening of the struggle through the formation of rank-and-file strike committees.

business.title: Stocks to watch, March 30: Thermax, Coal India, ONGC, defence stocks, Oberoi Realty, Urban Company, Ceigall India
business.description: Stocks to watch: Defence stocks will be in focus as, in a major defence acquisition push, India on Friday cleared the procurement of a fresh batch of five S-400 missile systems from Russia and 60 medium transport aircraft as well as a few other military platforms at a total cost of ₹2.38 lakh crore to boost the combat prowess of the defence forces.

business.title: New drug sotatercept shows benefit in CpcPH-HFpEF patients
business.description: Patients who have heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF) and high blood pressure in the lungs (pulmonary hypertension) experienced significant improvements in blood pressure and vascular health after taking the drug sotatercept, according to a study presented at the American College of Cardiology's Annual Scientific Session (ACC.26).

business.title: Zetwerk files confidentially for IPO
business.description: Kotak Mahindra Capital, JM Financial, Avendus Capital, Pantomath Capital and the Indian units of HSBC, Morgan Stanley, and Goldman Sachs are bankers to the issue.

business.title: Trial shows mavacamten benefits adolescents with obstructive heart condition
business.description: Adolescent patients with obstructive hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM) who received the drug mavacamten saw a significant improvement in left ventricular outflow tract (LVOT) gradient, a measure of blood flow obstruction in the heart, compared with those who received a placebo, according to a small study presented at the American College of Cardiology's Annual Scientific Session (ACC.26).

business.title: Left-ventricular unloading during angioplasty fails to improve patient outcomes
business.description: The first randomized trial to compare standard percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI), also known as coronary angioplasty, with PCI accompanied by the use of a temporary, miniaturized pump to support the heart in patients with both severe coronary heart disease and moderate to severe heart failure found no significant difference between the two approaches.

science.title: One of Earth’s most explosive supervolcanoes is recharging
science.description: Far beneath the ocean near Japan, scientists have discovered that the magma system linked to the most powerful eruption of the Holocene is slowly rebuilding. By using seismic imaging, researchers mapped a large magma reservoir under the Kikai caldera and confirmed it is the same system that fueled the massive eruption 7,300 years ago. However, the magma now present is newly injected, not leftover, as shown by changes in the chemistry of recent volcanic material and the growth of a lava dome over thousands of years.

science.title: Liquids behave like solids when stress builds faster than flow
science.description: Researchers found liquids can fracture like solids under specific conditions, producing sudden snapping sounds similar to those heard when solid materials break

science.title: Is Our Love Affair With The Moon A Thing Of The Past?
science.description: NASA’s announcement that they were looking to build a permanent base on the moon by 2036 hardly caused a ripple of international excitement.

science.description: 'It’s like pushing a merry-go-round,' said one of the scientists who studied recent data on Jupiter-family comet 41P/Tuttle–Giacobini–Kresak.

science.title: A spatiotemporal transcriptomic atlas of porcine (Sus scrofa) female early gonadal development
science.description: The early gonads of mammals contain primordial germ cells (PGCs) and gonadal somatic cells. In females, the gonadal somatic cells promote the specification of PGCs into oogonia and their entry into meiosis, which is crucial for oogenesis. Although single-cell transcriptome sequencing technology holds significant advantages in cell type identification, its inability to resolve spatial positional information substantially hampers research on communication mechanisms between germ cells and adjacent somatic cells. Here, we utilized high-resolution spatial transcriptomic technology to dissect the spatial dynamics of various cell types during the specification of PGCs into oogonia and their entry into meiosis in porcine gonads. We clarified the spatial localization of two waves of granulosa cells in the supporting cell lineage and their roles in regulating germ cell development. Furthermore, we found that interstitial and endothelial cells were predominantly located in the medullary region of the early gonads. Notably, cell-cell communication analysis revealed the critical role of BMP signaling (BMP2, BMP4 and GDF5) in driving the specification of PGCs into oogonia and their subsequent entry into meiosis. Our study provides a spatially resolved understanding of the PGC-to-oogonia specification in vivo and offers critical resources for reconstituting oogenesis in vitro. A study presents a spatial transcriptomic atlas of porcine fetal ovaries, revealing how germ cells develop and interact with surrounding somatic cells. Key findings highlight the spatial organization of germ cells and granulosa cells and the critical role of BMP signaling in guiding early oogenesis.

ai.title: AI chatbots may be making users more self-centred, warns Stanford study on ‘sycophancy’ risks

ai.title: Chinese company to use AI-powered chromosome testing to boost profits and fast track IVF
ai.description: China’s falling fertility rate sees spike in demand for assisted reproduction as biotech firm raises US$101 million after Hong Kong listing.

ai.title: Dubious AI detectors drive 'pay-to-humanize' scam
ai.description: WASHINGTON - Feed an Iranian news dispatch or a literary classic into some text detectors, and they return the same verdict: AI-generated. Then comes the pitch: pay to "humanize" the writing, a pattern experts say bears the hallmarks of a scam.

ai.title: AI-based tool matches standard method for coronary blood flow assessment
ai.description: A novel, minimally invasive computer software-based method that uses artificial intelligence to determine whether plaques in a coronary artery are restricting blood flow to the patient's heart performed similarly to the standard, more invasive wire-based procedure, according to a large international randomized trial presented at the American College of Cardiology's Annual Scientific Session (ACC.26).

ai.title: AI leadership in Southeast Asia: rethinking how we lead
ai.description: Artificial intelligence is no longer something we are preparing for. It is already here — reshaping how organisations operate, compete and make decisions.

ai.title: Will education ever become free? OpenAI investor Vinod Khosla predicts it will
ai.description: With millions accessing free courses online, education is being redefined. OpenAI investor Vinod Khosla argued that AI could make tutors and expertise nearly free. That idea now looks less like a distant bet and more like a shift already taking shape online.
